title: Gold Evidence Map for Generic Procurement Contract MVP
description: Maps gold ontology concepts, attributes, relations, events, rules and actions to input dataset sources. This is not an acceptable aliases file, known conflicts file, or scoring script.

evidence.evidence_summary: Department table defines organizational units with department code, name, cost center, active flag and created timestamp.
User table defines internal users/employees, role code, department reference, email, active flag and role code check constraint.
Supplier table defines supplier code, name, supplier status, risk rating, contact email and onboarding timestamp.
Purchase request table defines request number, requester, department, preferred supplier, amount, status, justification and lifecycle timestamps.
Request status check constraint enumerates purchase request lifecycle states.
DDL comment states that PR amount >= 50000 requires finance manager approval.
Purchase request line table defines line-level item description, category, quantity, unit, estimated unit price and line amount.
Approval record table defines target type/id, step name, approver, status, comments and decision timestamps.
Approval status check constraint enumerates approval lifecycle states.
Contract table defines contract number, request, supplier, amount, status, term dates, legal reviewer, legal approval and activation timestamps.
Contract status check constraint enumerates contract lifecycle states.
Purchase order table references contract, request and supplier and contains PO amount, status, release and close timestamps.
Purchase order status check constraint includes released and partially received states.
Goods receipt table references purchase order and receiver, with receipt status, received time, inspection result and remarks.
Receipt status check constraint enumerates goods receipt lifecycle states.
Invoice table references supplier, purchase order and goods receipt and stores invoice amount, date, status, variance and matched timestamp.
Invoice status check constraint enumerates invoice matching lifecycle states.
Payment request table references invoice and stores payment amount, status, due date, approver and payment timestamps.
Payment status check constraint enumerates payment lifecycle states.
PurchaseRequest domain class defines request attributes, editability and finance review rule.
Submit PR logic requires editable status, at least one line and business justification before submission.
Cancel PR logic blocks normal cancellation of approved PR and requires cancellation reason.
Approval service routes requests over 50000 to finance manager and other requests to department manager.
Approval service validates whether approver role has permission for the PR amount.
Rejection logic requires explanatory comment with minimum length.
Supplier domain class defines supplier attributes and eligibility rule for active and non-high-risk suppliers.
Contract domain class defines contract fields including request, supplier, amount, status, legal approval and activation timestamps.
Contract creation checks vendor/supplier eligibility and creates contract from approved PR context.
Only draft contracts can enter legal review.
Contract activation requires legal approval and contract term dates, then sets activation timestamp.
Contract termination requires a reason.
PO release is allowed only when the related contract is active.
GoodsReceipt domain class defines receipt number, PO reference, status, receiver, received time, inspection result and remarks.
Recording goods receipt requires received time.

evidence.source_location: matchInvoice(PurchaseOrder order, GoodsReceipt receipt, Invoice invoice)
evidence.evidence_summary: Invoice matching requires goods receipt and checks invoice amount variance against a tolerance rate.
Invoice domain class defines invoice number, supplier, PO, receipt, amount, date, status, variance and matched timestamp.
PaymentRequest domain class defines payment number, invoice, amount, status, due date, approver and paid timestamp.
Payment application can be created only for matched invoice.
Only finance manager or procurement admin can approve payment.
Only approved payment request can be marked paid.
Status constants define PR, contract, PO, invoice and payment states.
Role constants define requester, manager, procurement, finance, legal, warehouse, AP and admin roles.
Department sample rows provide department codes, names and cost centers.
User sample rows provide employees across requester, manager, procurement, finance, legal, warehouse, AP and admin roles.
Supplier sample rows show active, suspended and pending review suppliers with low, medium and high risk ratings.
Purchase request sample rows cover draft, submitted, approved, rejected and cancelled statuses with amounts and timestamps.
Request line sample rows show item descriptions, categories, quantities, units, unit prices and line amounts.
Approval sample rows cover department approval, finance approval, legal review and payment approval with pending, approved and rejected statuses.
Contract sample rows cover active, legal review, draft, expired and terminated contracts with legal approval and activation timestamps.
Purchase order sample rows show released, partially received, closed and cancelled orders linked to contracts, requests and suppliers.
Goods receipt sample rows include GRN numbers, pending, partial, complete and rejected receipt statuses and inspection outcomes.
Invoice sample rows cover matched, pending match, exception and cancelled invoices with match variance amounts.
Payment request sample rows cover requested, approved, paid, held and cancelled payment statuses.
Business overview lists core objects: Purchase Request, line, Supplier, Approval Record, Contract, Purchase Order, Goods Receipt, Invoice, Payment Request, Department and User.
End-to-end flow describes request creation, approval, supplier confirmation, contract, PO, goods receipt, invoice matching and payment.
Procurement process document defines DRAFT, SUBMITTED, APPROVED, REJECTED and CANCELLED request status transitions.
Approval flow states approval thresholds, manager and finance approval requirements, and rejection/cancellation reason requirements.
Supplier selection states procurement must confirm supplier is active and not high risk before award.
Purchase order can be released only after related contract is active and one purchase request may lead to multiple purchase orders.
Contract is a legal agreement with a supplier, normally created from an approved purchase request.
Contract lifecycle document defines DRAFT to LEGAL_REVIEW to ACTIVE to EXPIRED or TERMINATED.
Contract rules require request, supplier, legal approval before activation, supplier risk control and termination reason.
Receipt policy defines Goods Receipt/GRN and PENDING, PARTIAL, COMPLETE, REJECTED status flow.
Invoice matching policy defines invoice status flow, PO and goods receipt matching, payment block before match and variance tolerance.
Payment policy defines Payment Request/Payment Application status flow and finance approval before payment.
Role matrix defines requester, department manager, procurement specialist, finance manager, legal reviewer, warehouse clerk, AP clerk and procurement admin.
Permission matrix maps roles to actions such as create request, approve request, select supplier, draft contract, record receipt, match invoice and approve payment.
Business rules state PR requires line item and justification, amount non-negative, approved PR cannot be cancelled normally, and rejected requests may be resubmitted.

evidence.source_location: ## Supplier Rules; ## Contract Rules; ## Purchase Order Rules
evidence.evidence_summary: Business rules state supplier must be active, high-risk suppliers should not be selected, contract requires legal approval and PO requires active contract.
Business rules define invoice matching, variance tolerance, matched invoice requirement before payment approval and paid timestamp.
Glossary lists preferred terms and other terms: Purchase Request/PR, Supplier/Vendor, PO, Goods Receipt/GRN, Payment Request/Payment Application, and Contract/Agreement.
Permission matrix provides allowed roles for action create_purchase_request.
Permission matrix provides allowed roles for action submit_purchase_request.
Permission matrix provides allowed roles for action approve_normal_purchase_request.
Permission matrix provides allowed roles for action approve_high_value_purchase_request.
Permission matrix provides allowed roles for action select_supplier.
Permission matrix provides allowed roles for action draft_contract.
Permission matrix provides allowed roles for action legal_review_contract.
Permission matrix provides allowed roles for action activate_contract.
Permission matrix provides allowed roles for action release_purchase_order.
Permission matrix provides allowed roles for action record_goods_receipt.
Permission matrix provides allowed roles for action match_invoice.
Permission matrix provides allowed roles for action create_payment_request.
Permission matrix provides allowed roles for action approve_payment.
Permission matrix provides allowed roles for action mark_payment_paid.

usage_notes: Use evidence supports to check whether an AI output cites or can be traced to the same input sources as the gold ontology element.
One evidence item may support multiple ontology elements, and one ontology element may be supported by multiple evidence items.
Evidence IDs are stable references for manual review and future scoring rules, but this file does not implement scoring.
